purpose: Headless verification harness for the Phase 6 Bundle A3 Enrichment Center Credits feature. Promoted from /tmp/a3-verify.js into the repo so it survives machine changes and can grow with future Bundle A items. Drives Playwright + Chromium against a localhost static server (default http://localhost:8765) and runs three suites in sequence: (1) launcherTests — DOM render, autosave, mode toggle, char counter, 2,500-char threshold visibility, CLEAR, hydration after reload, ?random=1 redirect, paste-truncation notice; (2) variantOverrideTests — for each of portal/, portal2/, portal2/portal1style/, seeds localStorage and checks cake.useNaturalDelay / cake.customCreditsLoop / window.credits across 4 scenarios (EMPTY, SHORT+LOOP, SHORT+STOP, LONG); (3) scrollSmokeTests — dismisses the splash and waits per-variant for credit characters to actually appear in #creditstext, catching bugs the freeze-frame assertions can't (e.g. cake.init runs but typing never starts). Exit code = number of failed assertions; 0 on green.
calls_out_to: page.goto / reload / evaluate / fill / click / locator
invariants: BASE env var overrides the default http://localhost:8765; the preflight() fetch fails loud with exit code 2 and a python3 -m http.server hint if the server is not reachable, so the harness never burns time launching chromium against a dead origin.

Every scenario uses a fresh browser context (browser.newContext()) so localStorage is isolated across tests — without this, the persisted custom-credits state from one assertion would leak into the next.

Variant override tests rely on cake.useNaturalDelay / cake.customCreditsLoop being set synchronously inside the DOMContentLoaded applyCustomCredits handler, BEFORE splash dismissal. The waitForFunction(() => window.cake && window.credits) gate exists only to ensure DOMContentLoaded has run, not to wait for cake.init.

Scroll smoke per-variant wait times match each cake.js's creditsStartTime: portal/ uses creditsStartTime=9 so the smoke waits 12s; portal2/ and portal2/portal1style/ both use creditsStartTime=0 so 4s is enough. The autoplay-policy=no-user-gesture-required Chromium flag lets the splash click (the gesture) start audio + lyric typing in headless mode.

Credit-typing assertion strips both the blinker character ('_') and U+0020 spaces from #creditstext.textContent — only NON-blinker, NON-space characters proving real credit chars were typed. The seeded custom credits intentionally contain no '_' or ' ' chars so the regex is safe.

Each variant override scenario asserts exactly 3 cake-state values + (where relevant) a credits-array shape check, summing to the assertion count documented in the HANDOFF (~52). Adding new scenarios should keep the same shape so the count remains predictable.
gotchas: Requires `npm install` inside test/ to pull playwright + `npx playwright install chromium` for the ~113 MB headless-shell binary. Both are one-time per machine. test/node_modules and test/package-lock.json are gitignored.

page.url() after the ?random=1 redirect can resolve to any of the three variants — the assertion accepts the union and just verifies the URL matches /(portal|portal2|portal2\/portal1style)\/.+autoloop=1.+random=1/. Tightening this to a specific variant would break randomness.

Paste-truncation test dispatches a synthetic InputEvent with inputType='insertFromPaste' — this matches what real browsers send for paste, but headless-shell may differ across major versions. If this assertion ever flakes, check that the InputEvent constructor still honors the inputType field in the running Chromium.

The scroll smoke gives portal/ a hard 12s budget. If portal/cake.js's creditsStartTime is ever lowered, the test will pass earlier but the wait will still burn 12s — adjust the wait constant in the variants array (top of scrollSmokeTests) if you want faster runs.

The LONG scenario seeds 300 repetitions of 'credit line\n' (3,600 chars) which crosses the 2,500 LOOP_THRESHOLD; if LOOP_THRESHOLD ever changes (currently in launcher/launcher.js line 25 AND in all three cake.js applyCustomCredits handlers), the seed length needs to grow correspondingly.

Headless chromium uses --mute-audio so the credits song doesn't blare during local runs; the autoplay-policy=no-user-gesture-required flag bypasses the autoplay gate that the splash exists to satisfy. Without these flags the scroll smoke would either fail (autoplay block) or be loud.

key_functions.name: assert
key_functions.summary: Increments pass/fail counters, prints status with optional detail, and pushes failures into a list reported at the end.

key_functions.name: preflight
key_functions.summary: Fetches /launcher/index.html before launching the browser; bails out with exit 2 and a server-start hint if the static server isn't reachable.

key_functions.name: launcherTests
key_functions.summary: Drives the launcher page through DOM render, autosave, mode toggle, threshold-based fieldset visibility, CLEAR, hydration-after-reload, ?random=1 redirect, and paste-truncation notice.

key_functions.name: variantOverrideTests
key_functions.summary: For each of the three variants, runs 4 isolated scenarios (EMPTY, SHORT+LOOP, SHORT+STOP, LONG) seeding localStorage and asserting cake.useNaturalDelay / cake.customCreditsLoop / window.credits after DOMContentLoaded.

key_functions.name: scrollSmokeTests
key_functions.summary: Real-time assertion: dismisses each variant's splash and waits per-variant for credit characters to actually appear in #creditstext, catching bugs where cake.init runs but typing fails to start.
